We weren't quite sure what to expect. Had some wine at Stanleys across the street. Highly recommended. Strongly suggest researching before going. Still not sure what this was but many many rooms with early century beliefs, cures, tech? Upstairs garden with ginger snaps, tea and birds.

This might barely cling to a 3 rating but I'd rather you didn't spend time on this and do something else instead.  I'm into quirky, and I carefully read reviews (good and bad) here and decided I would give it go.  I wanted it to succeed.  Sadly, no.  Maybe if they had allowed photos (prohibited).  A lot of this is random curiosities, some of it is just made up (though occasionally the tongue in cheek captions were pretty good), a few things are genuinely interesting.  There is no flow or rhythm, but that's a quibble.  There just isn't a lot quality or museum professionalism on display.  The idea - a cabinet of curiosities - is decent, the execution just does not live up.  And the staff are completely nonplussed with you for having shown up.  My last somewhat random thought - as an insurance professional - 'firetrap' good luck getting out quickly enough.  Overall a disappointment and I'm very glad I visited this as a tack on rather than sacrificed another attraction for it - do NOT make this a focus for the day.

My friend suggested that we meet at MOJT for a weekend date.  I parked on the street and easily spent several hours perusing every single corner of this one of a kind cabinet of curiosities.  I had to return several times to feed the meter because there was so much to see. I am a museum goer and this was so different than all others.  
We made reservations and spent several hours enjoying all room that the clever creators put together for our enjoyment.  I will return with other friends to enjoy this sweet, charming, reprise. Picture taking is discouraged and I appreciate the owners for their creativity and trusting us with your precious displays.
